composite-bonding.title: Same-Day Repairs. No Drilling, No Anesthesia.
composite-bonding.subheading: Composite bonding sculpts tooth-coloured resin onto your existing tooth — fixing chips, gaps and small cosmetic concerns in a single visit, with zero drilling.

sections.heading: Why Bonding Is the Best 'Quick Win' in Dentistry
The Real Benefits Patients Notice
From First Visit to Finished Smile
Bonding Is Right For You If You Have Small Cosmetic Concerns
Common Questions About Composite Bonding
Chip or gap? Fixed in 60 minutes.

paragraphs: Composite bonding sculpts tooth-coloured resin onto your existing tooth — fixing chips, gaps and small cosmetic concerns in a single visit, with zero drilling.

It's the most affordable cosmetic option, doesn't require enamel removal, and can be completed in a single visit. Perfect for fixing a chipped front tooth, closing a small gap, or reshaping an uneven tooth.

30–60 minutes per tooth No drilling, no anaesthetic needed Same-day results Most affordable cosmetic option Why It Matters The Real Benefits Patients Notice Same-Day Fix Walk in with a chipped tooth, walk out with it repaired — 60 minutes start to finish.
Most Affordable Far less expensive than veneers or crowns — ideal for minor cosmetic concerns.
Tooth-Conservative No enamel is removed — your natural tooth structure stays intact.
Beautiful Aesthetic Composite is hand-shaped and polished to match your surrounding teeth.
The Process From First Visit to Finished Smile 01 01 Shade Match We select composite shade to match your existing tooth colour exactly.
Light etching of the surface creates a microscopic texture for the composite to bond to.
Composite is layered, shaped freehand, and cured with a special blue light.
Final sanding and high polish leave a finish indistinguishable from natural enamel.

Is This Right For You? Bonding Is Right For You If You Have Small Cosmetic Concerns Bonding excels at small problems — chips, minor gaps, slight reshaping. For larger transformations, veneers or crowns are usually better.

Chipped or worn front tooth Small gap between front teeth Slightly misshapen or uneven tooth Minor surface discoloration Receding gum exposing root Want a same-day, affordable fix Frequently Asked Common Questions About Composite Bonding Bonding vs. veneers — which should I choose? Bonding is best for one or two teeth or small fixes — cheaper, faster, no enamel removal. Veneers are better for multiple teeth or major colour/shape changes — last longer and look slightly more refined.
5–8 years for most patients. Lasts longer with regular cleanings, a nightguard if you grind, and avoiding biting hard objects like ice or pens.
Composite stains more than porcelain. Cut back on coffee, red wine and tobacco to keep it looking fresh — or budget for a polish every few years.
Yes — properly placed bonding is impossible to distinguish from natural enamel. We shade-match and polish carefully.
$200–$500 per tooth depending on size and complexity. A single front-tooth chip is typically $250.
Most patients pay $200–$400. Sometimes covered by insurance as a restorative procedure.
Chip or gap? Fixed in 60 minutes. Walk-in consults welcome. Most bonding is done same-visit — book today for a same-week appointment.

faqs.question: Bonding vs. veneers — which should I choose?
faqs.answer: Bonding is best for one or two teeth or small fixes — cheaper, faster, no enamel removal. Veneers are better for multiple teeth or major colour/shape changes — last longer and look slightly more refined.

faqs.question: How long does it last?
faqs.answer: 5–8 years for most patients. Lasts longer with regular cleanings, a nightguard if you grind, and avoiding biting hard objects like ice or pens.

faqs.question: Will it stain?
faqs.answer: Composite stains more than porcelain. Cut back on coffee, red wine and tobacco to keep it looking fresh — or budget for a polish every few years.

faqs.question: Does it look natural?
faqs.answer: Yes — properly placed bonding is impossible to distinguish from natural enamel. We shade-match and polish carefully.

faqs.question: How much does it cost?
faqs.answer: $200–$500 per tooth depending on size and complexity. A single front-tooth chip is typically $250.

pricing: 30–60 minutes per tooth No drilling, no anaesthetic needed Same-day results Most affordable cosmetic option Why It Matters The Real Benefits Patients Notice Same-Day Fix Walk in with a chipped tooth, walk out with it repaired — 60 minutes start to finish.
$200–$500 per tooth depending on size and complexity. A single front-tooth chip is typically $250.
Most patients pay $200–$400. Sometimes covered by insurance as a restorative procedure.

lumineers.title: Reversible Smile Makeover. No Drilling. No Shots.
lumineers.subheading: Lumineers are ultra-thin, no-prep porcelain veneers that bond directly over your teeth — no shaving, no needles, and 100% reversible. Beautiful, fast, conservative.

sections.heading: Lumineers vs. Traditional Veneers
The Real Benefits Patients Notice
From First Visit to Finished Smile
Lumineers Are Ideal For Cosmetic Improvement Without Commitment
Common Questions About Lumineers
Try a reversible smile transformation

paragraphs: Lumineers are ultra-thin, no-prep porcelain veneers that bond directly over your teeth — no shaving, no needles, and 100% reversible. Beautiful, fast, conservative.

If you ever want them removed, your natural teeth are exactly as they were. Lumineers are the most conservative cosmetic option in dentistry — perfect for patients hesitant to permanently alter their teeth.

Zero drilling — no anaesthetic needed Fully reversible procedure Stain-resistant porcelain Lasts 10–20 years Why It Matters The Real Benefits Patients Notice Preserves Your Teeth Zero enamel removal means your natural teeth stay 100% intact under the Lumineers.
No Needles No drilling means no anaesthetic shots — the whole prep visit is needle-free.
Beautiful, Subtle Results Hand-crafted porcelain delivers a natural-looking improvement, not an obvious 'veneer look'.
Reversible Anytime Don't love them in 5 years? They can be removed and your natural teeth are unchanged.
The Process From First Visit to Finished Smile 01 01 Smile Design & Simulation Photos, digital simulation, and discussion of which teeth and what shade.
No goopy moulds — quick digital scans capture exact tooth shape.
Master lab hand-crafts each Lumineer to fit precisely over your existing teeth.
Lumineers cemented in a single comfortable visit. No prep, no shots, instant new smile.
Is This Right For You? Lumineers Are Ideal For Cosmetic Improvement Without Commitment If you love the idea of veneers but the permanence makes you hesitant — Lumineers are designed for you.

Hesitant to permanently alter teeth Minor cosmetic concerns (colour, small gaps) Teeth that are slightly small or misshapen Patients with sensitive teeth (no shots needed) Anyone wanting a subtle, natural enhancement Open to the slightly bulkier feel vs. traditional veneers Frequently Asked Common Questions About Lumineers Lumineers vs. traditional veneers — which should I choose? Lumineers if you want reversibility and minimal preparation. Traditional veneers if you want a more dramatic transformation or have significantly damaged front teeth. The free consult helps determine which fits your goals.
Lumineers add 0.2mm of porcelain to the front of your teeth — barely noticeable. Most patients adjust within a few days. Traditional veneers feel slightly thinner since enamel is removed first.
For minor-to-moderate cosmetic concerns: yes, essentially identical. For severe staining or major reshaping, traditional veneers give the dentist more control.
10–20 years with proper care — same as traditional veneers. Use a nightguard if you grind, avoid biting hard objects, maintain regular cleanings.
Roughly similar — $1,200–$1,500 per Lumineer. The 'savings' aren't financial; they're in tooth preservation and reversibility.
Financing available. Free smile simulation at consult.
Try a reversible smile transformation Free Lumineers consultation includes digital smile simulation — see your potential new smile before committing.
list_items: Teeth that are slightly small or misshapen
Patients with sensitive teeth (no shots needed)
Anyone wanting a subtle, natural enhancement
Open to the slightly bulkier feel vs. traditional veneers

faqs.question: Lumineers vs. traditional veneers — which should I choose?
faqs.answer: Lumineers if you want reversibility and minimal preparation. Traditional veneers if you want a more dramatic transformation or have significantly damaged front teeth. The free consult helps determine which fits your goals.

faqs.question: Do they feel bulky?
faqs.answer: Lumineers add 0.2mm of porcelain to the front of your teeth — barely noticeable. Most patients adjust within a few days. Traditional veneers feel slightly thinner since enamel is removed first.

faqs.question: Will they look as good as traditional veneers?
faqs.answer: For minor-to-moderate cosmetic concerns: yes, essentially identical. For severe staining or major reshaping, traditional veneers give the dentist more control.

faqs.question: How long do Lumineers last?
faqs.answer: 10–20 years with proper care — same as traditional veneers. Use a nightguard if you grind, avoid biting hard objects, maintain regular cleanings.

faqs.question: Cost vs. traditional veneers?
faqs.answer: Roughly similar — $1,200–$1,500 per Lumineer. The 'savings' aren't financial; they're in tooth preservation and reversibility.

pricing: Roughly similar — $1,200–$1,500 per Lumineer. The 'savings' aren't financial; they're in tooth preservation and reversibility.

teeth-whitening.title: 5–8 Shades Brighter, Without Damaging Your Enamel
teeth-whitening.subheading: Coffee, wine, tea and time leave their marks. One in-office whitening session removes years of staining safely — far beyond what drugstore strips can deliver.

sections.heading: The Truth About Over-the-Counter Whitening Strips
The Real Benefits Patients Notice
From First Visit to Finished Smile
Whitening Works Best For Healthy, Stained Teeth
Common Questions About Teeth Whitening
Brighter teeth in 60 minutes

paragraphs: Coffee, wine, tea and time leave their marks. One in-office whitening session removes years of staining safely — far beyond what drugstore strips can deliver.
Our in-office system uses pharmacy-grade peroxide with a custom gum barrier, achieving in 60 minutes what would take you 4–6 weeks of nightly strips — and far whiter.

Pharmacy-grade peroxide Custom gum barrier prevents burns Take-home trays for touch-ups Safe for enamel — no damage Why It Matters The Real Benefits Patients Notice Dramatic Results 5–8 shades whiter in a single 60-minute visit. Visible from across the room.
Enamel-Safe Unlike harsh DIY methods (charcoal, lemon, baking soda) our system protects your enamel.
Fast & Convenient One lunch break and you're done. Take-home trays let you maintain the result for years.
Reduced Sensitivity Our protocol includes desensitizer — most patients report little to no post-whitening sensitivity.
The Process From First Visit to Finished Smile 01 01 Pre-Whitening Cleaning We start with a professional cleaning — whitening works best on plaque-free teeth.
A custom protective barrier is applied so the gel doesn't touch your gums.
Three 15-minute applications of pharmacy-grade gel, with a special activating light.
Custom trays + touch-up gel so you can maintain the result for the next 1–2 years.

Is This Right For You? Whitening Works Best For Healthy, Stained Teeth Whitening corrects surface and deep stains — but works only on natural tooth structure (not crowns, veneers or fillings, which keep their original colour).

Yellow or stained teeth from coffee/tea/wine Age-related dulling of enamel Smokers (after they've quit) Pre-wedding, reunion or new job Healthy gums and no untreated cavities Patients realistic about results (won't bleach crowns or veneers) Frequently Asked Common Questions About Teeth Whitening Is whitening safe? Yes — when professionally administered. The hydrogen peroxide briefly opens enamel pores to lift stains, then your saliva remineralizes them. Decades of research confirm it's safe.
Most patients report mild sensitivity to cold for 24–48 hours after. Our protocol includes a desensitizing application that minimizes this significantly.
1–2 years for most patients. Smokers and heavy coffee/wine drinkers see fading faster. Touch-up trays at home extend results indefinitely.
No — whitening only works on natural tooth enamel. If you have visible crowns or veneers in your smile, we'll plan accordingly (sometimes replacing them with new whiter ones is the right call).
Strips can absolutely whiten — slowly. But they often slip, irritate gums, and max out at 1–2 shades. In-office whitening achieves 5–8 shades safely. Your call on time vs. money.
Includes custom trays for touch-ups. Often offered in promotional packages.
Brighter teeth in 60 minutes Book your whitening visit and walk out 5–8 shades whiter. Take-home trays included for ongoing maintenance.
list_items: Yellow or stained teeth from coffee/tea/wine
Healthy gums and no untreated cavities
Patients realistic about results (won't bleach crowns or veneers)

faqs.question: Is whitening safe?
faqs.answer: Yes — when professionally administered. The hydrogen peroxide briefly opens enamel pores to lift stains, then your saliva remineralizes them. Decades of research confirm it's safe.

faqs.question: Does it hurt?
faqs.answer: Most patients report mild sensitivity to cold for 24–48 hours after. Our protocol includes a desensitizing application that minimizes this significantly.

faqs.question: How long do results last?
faqs.answer: 1–2 years for most patients. Smokers and heavy coffee/wine drinkers see fading faster. Touch-up trays at home extend results indefinitely.

faqs.question: Will it whiten my crowns or veneers?
faqs.answer: No — whitening only works on natural tooth enamel. If you have visible crowns or veneers in your smile, we'll plan accordingly (sometimes replacing them with new whiter ones is the right call).

faqs.question: Why not just use drugstore strips?
faqs.answer: Strips can absolutely whiten — slowly. But they often slip, irritate gums, and max out at 1–2 shades. In-office whitening achieves 5–8 shades safely. Your call on time vs. money.

orthodontics.title: Straight Teeth for Every Age & Lifestyle
orthodontics.subheading: From your child's first orthodontic check to discreet adult treatment — we offer the full range: traditional braces, clear aligners, and early intervention. Free consults always.

sections.heading: Three Paths to a Straighter Smile
The Real Benefits Patients Notice
From First Visit to Finished Smile
Orthodontics Helps Every Age & Stage
Common Questions About Orthodontics
Free orthodontic consultation

paragraphs: From your child's first orthodontic check to discreet adult treatment — we offer the full range: traditional braces, clear aligners, and early intervention. Free consults always.
Your treatment is chosen based on the complexity of your case, your age, and your lifestyle — not a one-size-fits-all approach. Free consults include digital photos and a custom recommendation.

Traditional + ceramic braces Invisalign clear aligners Phase 1 (early-intervention) treatment Adult-friendly discreet options Why It Matters The Real Benefits Patients Notice Confidence Boost Patients consistently report dramatic confidence gains after orthodontic treatment.
Easier to Clean Straight teeth are far easier to floss and brush — lowering long-term decay and gum disease risk.
Better Bite Function Aligned teeth bite, chew and speak more efficiently — reducing jaw strain over time.
Lifelong Investment Orthodontic results last a lifetime with proper retainer use.
The Process From First Visit to Finished Smile 01 01 Free Consultation Digital photos, X-rays, and a recommendation for the right approach (braces, aligners, or phase 1).
3D scans map your bite. We design every tooth movement and show you a simulation of the final result.
Visits every 6–8 weeks for adjustments. Most cases complete in 12–24 months.
Custom retainers keep your new smile in place. We make replacement retainers as needed.

Is This Right For You? Orthodontics Helps Every Age & Stage First orthodontic check should happen by age 7. But adults of any age benefit from treatment — we've successfully straightened smiles from 8 to 78.

Crooked, crowded or rotated teeth Overbite, underbite or crossbite Gaps between teeth Children with developing alignment issues (age 7+) Teens before high school photos Adults considering Invisalign for discretion Frequently Asked Common Questions About Orthodontics What's the best age to start orthodontics? First check at age 7. Most active treatment starts between 10–14, but adult treatment is increasingly common. There's truly no age limit.
Both work brilliantly. Braces handle complex cases better. Invisalign offers superior discretion and removability. Free consult includes recommendation for your case.
$3,500–$7,500 depending on complexity. Most plans cover up to 50% of orthodontic costs for kids and teens. Adult orthodontic insurance is rarer but available.
There's mild discomfort for 2–3 days after each adjustment — easily managed with over-the-counter pain relief. Most patients are comfortable within a week of starting.
Subtle changes within 6–8 weeks. Major visible progress around the 6-month mark. Full treatment 12–24 months for most cases.
Most insurance plans cover up to 50%. 0% financing available.
Free orthodontic consultation Bring your kids or yourself. 30-minute consult includes digital photos, X-rays and a clear treatment recommendation — no obligation.

faqs.question: What's the best age to start orthodontics?
faqs.answer: First check at age 7. Most active treatment starts between 10–14, but adult treatment is increasingly common. There's truly no age limit.

faqs.question: Braces vs. Invisalign — which is better?
faqs.answer: Both work brilliantly. Braces handle complex cases better. Invisalign offers superior discretion and removability. Free consult includes recommendation for your case.

faqs.question: How much does it cost?
faqs.answer: $3,500–$7,500 depending on complexity. Most plans cover up to 50% of orthodontic costs for kids and teens. Adult orthodontic insurance is rarer but available.

faqs.question: Will it hurt?
faqs.answer: There's mild discomfort for 2–3 days after each adjustment — easily managed with over-the-counter pain relief. Most patients are comfortable within a week of starting.

faqs.question: How long until I see results?
faqs.answer: Subtle changes within 6–8 weeks. Major visible progress around the 6-month mark. Full treatment 12–24 months for most cases.

pricing: $3,500–$7,500 depending on complexity. Most plans cover up to 50% of orthodontic costs for kids and teens. Adult orthodontic insurance is rarer but available.

invisalign.title: Straighten Your Teeth Without Anyone Knowing
invisalign.subheading: Invisalign uses a series of clear, removable aligners to gradually shift your teeth into place — discreet, comfortable and removable for eating, brushing and special occasions.

sections.heading: Why Adults Choose Invisalign Over Traditional Braces
The Real Benefits Patients Notice
From First Visit to Finished Smile
Invisalign Works For Most Orthodontic Cases
Common Questions About Invisalign Clear Braces
Free Invisalign simulation & consult

paragraphs: Invisalign uses a series of clear, removable aligners to gradually shift your teeth into place — discreet, comfortable and removable for eating, brushing and special occasions.

As an Invisalign Platinum Provider, we've completed hundreds of cases — including complex ones that other clinics refer out. The first consult is always free and includes a digital simulation of your finished smile.

Virtually invisible aligners Removable for eating & brushing Fewer office visits than braces No diet restrictions Why It Matters The Real Benefits Patients Notice Practically Invisible Most people won't notice you're wearing them — perfect for adults in client-facing roles.
Removable Anytime Take them out for meals, photos, special events. Just 20–22 hours daily for best results.
No Diet Restrictions Unlike braces, you can eat anything — popcorn, gum, apples, sticky candy.
Fewer Visits 6–8 week check-ins instead of monthly braces tightenings. Saves time over the year.
The Process From First Visit to Finished Smile 01 01 Free Smile Simulation 3D scan + computer simulation shows your finished smile before treatment starts.
Custom-fit aligners ship to us in 3–4 weeks. We deliver several sets at each visit.
Wear each set 1–2 weeks, 20–22 hours daily. Switch to the next set as scheduled.
Final fine-tuning, then custom retainers to keep your perfect new smile in place.

Is This Right For You? Invisalign Works For Most Orthodontic Cases Invisalign can correct most issues that traditional braces fix — and is especially popular with adults, teens, and anyone wanting a discreet treatment option.

Crooked, crowded, or rotated teeth Mild to moderate overbite or underbite Gaps and spaces between teeth Adults wanting discretion in treatment Teens (Invisalign Teen available) Disciplined patients (will wear 20–22 hrs daily) Frequently Asked Common Questions About Invisalign Clear Braces How long does Invisalign take? 12–18 months for most adult cases. Simple cases finish in 6 months; complex ones take up to 24. The free consult includes a personalized estimate.
Mild pressure for 2–3 days at each new set as teeth shift. Most patients adapt within a week per tray. No metal wires poking your cheeks.
Some patients have a slight lisp for the first 3–5 days as their tongue adjusts. It goes away once you're used to the aligners — usually within a week.
Water only with aligners in. Coffee, tea, and dark drinks will stain them. Remove for any food or drink besides water, then brush before reinserting.
$5,000–$7,500 all-inclusive (consult, scans, aligners, refinements, retainers). Most insurance plans cover up to $1,500–$2,500 of orthodontics.
Includes consult, scans, all aligners, refinements and retainers. Financing available.
Free Invisalign simulation & consult 30-minute consult includes free 3D smile simulation — see your finished smile before committing to treatment.
list_items: Mild to moderate overbite or underbite

faqs.question: How long does Invisalign take?
faqs.answer: 12–18 months for most adult cases. Simple cases finish in 6 months; complex ones take up to 24. The free consult includes a personalized estimate.

faqs.question: Does it hurt?
faqs.answer: Mild pressure for 2–3 days at each new set as teeth shift. Most patients adapt within a week per tray. No metal wires poking your cheeks.

faqs.question: Will I lisp?
faqs.answer: Some patients have a slight lisp for the first 3–5 days as their tongue adjusts. It goes away once you're used to the aligners — usually within a week.

faqs.question: Can I drink coffee with them in?
faqs.answer: Water only with aligners in. Coffee, tea, and dark drinks will stain them. Remove for any food or drink besides water, then brush before reinserting.

faqs.question: How much does it cost?
faqs.answer: $5,000–$7,500 all-inclusive (consult, scans, aligners, refinements, retainers). Most insurance plans cover up to $1,500–$2,500 of orthodontics.

pricing: $5,000–$7,500 all-inclusive (consult, scans, aligners, refinements, retainers). Most insurance plans cover up to $1,500–$2,500 of orthodontics.

snap-on-smile.title: A Beautiful New Smile, Removable & Affordable
snap-on-smile.subheading: Snap-On Smile is a thin, custom-crafted appliance that fits over your existing teeth — instantly delivering a confident new smile without drilling, shots or permanent changes.

sections.heading: Snap-On Smile vs. Permanent Cosmetic Work
The Real Benefits Patients Notice
From First Visit to Finished Smile
Snap-On Smile Is Perfect For Patients Who Want Reversibility
Common Questions About Snap-On Smile
Try a new smile without commitment

paragraphs: Snap-On Smile is a thin, custom-crafted appliance that fits over your existing teeth — instantly delivering a confident new smile without drilling, shots or permanent changes.

It's the perfect choice for patients who want a beautiful smile but aren't ready (or can't afford) permanent veneers or implants. Wonderful for upcoming events, photo sessions, or as a long-term bridge until you're ready for permanent work.

No drilling or anaesthetic Removable anytime Eat and drink normally Fraction of veneer cost Why It Matters The Real Benefits Patients Notice Affordable Transformation About 1/4 the cost of a full set of veneers — life-changing aesthetic without the price tag.
Fast Turnaround Two visits, about three weeks total. Walk in with your existing smile, walk out with a new one.
Zero Tooth Damage Your underlying teeth are completely untouched — the appliance simply snaps over them.
Eat & Drink Normally Yes, you can eat and drink with it in. Just remove for cleaning and overnight.
The Process From First Visit to Finished Smile 01 01 Smile Design Consult Discuss shade, shape, and style. Take digital impressions of your existing teeth.
Snap-On Smile is custom-crafted to fit your bite — takes about three weeks.
We check fit, comfort and aesthetics. Minor adjustments at delivery for a perfect snap-on feel.
How to clean, store and maintain your Snap-On Smile for 5+ years of beautiful use.

Is This Right For You? Snap-On Smile Is Perfect For Patients Who Want Reversibility If you've been hesitant about veneers because of cost or permanence — Snap-On Smile lets you experience a beautiful new smile without commitment.

Upcoming wedding, reunion or photo shoot Budget concerns vs. veneers Hesitant about permanent dental work Missing teeth not yet replaced with implants Stained or worn teeth not amenable to whitening Want to 'preview' how veneers would look long-term Frequently Asked Common Questions About Snap-On Smile Will people notice it's not real? Up close, those who look carefully might. From conversational distance — no, they look like beautiful natural teeth. Many patients use Snap-On Smile for years before deciding to invest in permanent veneers.
Yes — soft foods absolutely, harder foods cautiously. Avoid biting directly into apples or steak with your front teeth. Remove for cleaning after meals.
5–8 years for most patients with proper care. Avoid biting hard objects, store in its case at night, and bring it for adjustment if anything feels off.
Typically the front 10 upper teeth (or 8 lower) — what shows when you smile. You can choose upper-only, lower-only, or both.
Generally no — Snap-On Smile is considered cosmetic. Cost is about $2,000–$3,500 per arch (upper or lower). Financing available.
Far less than veneers, and fully removable. Financing available.
Try a new smile without commitment Free Snap-On Smile consult: digital impressions and shade simulation. See your potential new smile before deciding.
list_items: Upcoming wedding, reunion or photo shoot
Missing teeth not yet replaced with implants
Stained or worn teeth not amenable to whitening
Want to 'preview' how veneers would look long-term

faqs.question: Will people notice it's not real?
faqs.answer: Up close, those who look carefully might. From conversational distance — no, they look like beautiful natural teeth. Many patients use Snap-On Smile for years before deciding to invest in permanent veneers.

faqs.question: Can I eat with it in?
faqs.answer: Yes — soft foods absolutely, harder foods cautiously. Avoid biting directly into apples or steak with your front teeth. Remove for cleaning after meals.

faqs.question: How long does it last?
faqs.answer: 5–8 years for most patients with proper care. Avoid biting hard objects, store in its case at night, and bring it for adjustment if anything feels off.

faqs.question: Does it cover all my teeth?
faqs.answer: Typically the front 10 upper teeth (or 8 lower) — what shows when you smile. You can choose upper-only, lower-only, or both.

faqs.question: Is it covered by insurance?
faqs.answer: Generally no — Snap-On Smile is considered cosmetic. Cost is about $2,000–$3,500 per arch (upper or lower). Financing available.

pricing: Generally no — Snap-On Smile is considered cosmetic. Cost is about $2,000–$3,500 per arch (upper or lower). Financing available.

tmj-therapy.title: Relief From Jaw Pain, Headaches & Nightly Grinding
tmj-therapy.subheading: TMJ disorders cause chronic headaches, jaw clicking, neck pain and sleep disruption. Our custom nightguards and physiotherapy-based approach get to the root — not just the symptoms.

sections.heading: How We Approach TMJ Differently
The Real Benefits Patients Notice
From First Visit to Finished Smile
You May Have a TMJ Disorder If
Common Questions About TMJ Therapy
Wake up without jaw pain

paragraphs: TMJ disorders cause chronic headaches, jaw clicking, neck pain and sleep disruption. Our custom nightguards and physiotherapy-based approach get to the root — not just the symptoms.

Our approach starts with a comprehensive assessment: bite analysis, joint imaging, muscle palpation and lifestyle review. Treatment is tailored: custom-fit nightguards, bite adjustment, jaw exercises, and referral to specialists when needed.

Comprehensive joint & bite assessment Custom-fit lab-made nightguard Bite adjustment when needed Coordinated with physio when beneficial Why It Matters The Real Benefits Patients Notice Headache Relief Most TMJ-related morning headaches resolve within 2–4 weeks of proper treatment.
Save Your Teeth Grinding wears teeth down rapidly. A custom guard protects them — saving thousands in future restorative work.
Better Sleep Reduced jaw clenching at night means deeper, more restorative sleep.
Long-Term Joint Health Untreated TMJ can permanently damage the jaw joint. Early treatment prevents this.
The Process From First Visit to Finished Smile 01 01 Comprehensive Assessment Joint palpation, bite analysis, X-rays, and detailed history of symptoms and triggers.
Together we identify your specific TMJ pattern: grinding, clenching, joint disorder, or bite imbalance.
Most often a custom nightguard. Sometimes bite adjustment, jaw exercises or specialist referral.
2-week and 6-week check-ins. We fine-tune until you're sleeping better and waking pain-free.
Is This Right For You? You May Have a TMJ Disorder If TMJ symptoms are often misattributed to migraines, stress or aging. If any of these sound familiar, a TMJ assessment is worth your time.

Wake up with jaw pain or headaches Clicking or popping when you open your mouth Teeth that are flat or worn-looking Neck or shoulder tension that won't resolve Partner reports grinding sounds at night Jaw locks open or shut occasionally Frequently Asked Common Questions About TMJ Therapy Will a drugstore nightguard work? Rarely — and sometimes it makes things worse. Drugstore guards are too thick and don't accommodate your specific bite, which can throw the joint further out of balance. Custom-fit is the only way to do this properly.
Most patients notice significant headache and jaw-pain reduction within 2–4 weeks of nightly guard use. Some cases (especially long-standing) take 2–3 months.
Custom nightguards are typically covered at 50–80% as a medical/dental device. We pre-determine before fabrication.
Most patients yes — grinding is usually a long-term pattern. The guard is comfortable enough that it becomes a normal part of bedtime.
Some cases need additional intervention — physiotherapy, Botox for muscle tension, or referral to a TMJ specialist. We coordinate the entire team if needed.
Most insurance plans cover 50–80%. Comprehensive assessment included.
Wake up without jaw pain Free 30-minute TMJ assessment. We'll evaluate your symptoms, examine your bite, and recommend the right next step — no commitment.
list_items: Wake up with jaw pain or headaches
Clicking or popping when you open your mouth
Teeth that are flat or worn-looking
Neck or shoulder tension that won't resolve
Partner reports grinding sounds at night
Jaw locks open or shut occasionally

faqs.question: Will a drugstore nightguard work?
faqs.answer: Rarely — and sometimes it makes things worse. Drugstore guards are too thick and don't accommodate your specific bite, which can throw the joint further out of balance. Custom-fit is the only way to do this properly.

faqs.question: How long until I feel better?
faqs.answer: Most patients notice significant headache and jaw-pain reduction within 2–4 weeks of nightly guard use. Some cases (especially long-standing) take 2–3 months.

faqs.question: Is it covered by insurance?
faqs.answer: Custom nightguards are typically covered at 50–80% as a medical/dental device. We pre-determine before fabrication.

faqs.question: Will I need to wear it forever?
faqs.answer: Most patients yes — grinding is usually a long-term pattern. The guard is comfortable enough that it becomes a normal part of bedtime.

faqs.question: What if a guard doesn't fix it?
faqs.answer: Some cases need additional intervention — physiotherapy, Botox for muscle tension, or referral to a TMJ specialist. We coordinate the entire team if needed.

sedation-dentistry.title: Sleep Through Your Dental Procedure — Stress-Free
sedation-dentistry.subheading: Whether you're anxious about the dentist or facing a complex procedure, our sedation options range from light relaxation to deep IV sedation. Wake up done — with little memory of the experience.

sections.heading: Three Sedation Options, Tailored to You
The Real Benefits Patients Notice
From First Visit to Finished Smile
Sedation Is Right For You If Dentistry Feels Overwhelming
Common Questions About Sedation Dentistry
End your dental anxiety for good

paragraphs: Whether you're anxious about the dentist or facing a complex procedure, our sedation options range from light relaxation to deep IV sedation. Wake up done — with little memory of the experience.

We offer three levels: nitrous oxide (light relaxation), oral sedation (you'll be very relaxed but awake), and IV sedation (deep sedation, minimal memory). Our team includes a certified anaesthesia provider for safe IV sedation in-office.

Nitrous oxide for light relaxation Oral sedation pills for moderate anxiety IV sedation for severe anxiety or complex work Certified anaesthesia team Why It Matters The Real Benefits Patients Notice End Dental Anxiety Many patients who avoided dentistry for years return to regular care after a positive sedation experience.
Multiple Procedures in One Visit Combine procedures (e.g., 4 fillings + a crown) into a single sedation visit — far less stressful.
Safe & Monitored Continuous vital-sign monitoring throughout. Our anaesthesia provider is certified and experienced.
Wake Up Done Most patients have only fragmented memory of the procedure — and zero anxiety afterward.
The Process From First Visit to Finished Smile 01 01 Sedation Consult We discuss your anxiety triggers, medical history, and which level of sedation fits best.
Fasting requirements, medication review, and arranging transportation home for oral/IV sedation.
Comfortable chair, warm blanket, soft music. Continuous monitoring. You'll feel calm or asleep depending on your sedation level.
30–60 minutes in our recovery area. A friend or family member drives you home — and you sleep off any residual effects comfortably.

Is This Right For You? Sedation Is Right For You If Dentistry Feels Overwhelming Sedation isn't only for the highly anxious — it's also ideal for long procedures, complex work, or anyone with a strong gag reflex or trouble staying still.

Severe dental anxiety or PTSD Avoided dentistry for years Complex procedure (multiple extractions, implants) Strong gag reflex Difficulty staying still (back pain, restlessness) Want multiple treatments combined into one visit Frequently Asked Common Questions About Sedation Dentistry Will I be unconscious? Depends on the level. Nitrous: fully awake, just relaxed. Oral sedation: very relaxed, may doze. IV sedation: deep sleep-like state, minimal memory. None are full general anaesthesia (we don't intubate).
Yes, when administered by a certified team with proper monitoring. Our IV sedation is overseen by a certified anaesthesia provider. We follow ASA monitoring guidelines for every patient.
For oral and IV sedation, a friend or family member must drive you home. You'll be relaxed but ambulatory after recovery (30–60 min). Don't drive, work, or sign legal documents for 24 hours.
Nitrous: $80. Oral sedation: $250. IV sedation: $400–$800 depending on duration. Some insurance plans cover sedation for documented anxiety or complex cases.
Nitrous wears off in 5 minutes. Oral sedation effects fade over 4–6 hours. IV sedation: most patients feel normal within 24 hours, though many sleep for several hours that afternoon.
Some plans cover sedation for documented anxiety. We discuss costs upfront.
End your dental anxiety for good Free consultation to discuss your anxiety, our sedation options, and a no-pressure plan to get you the care you need — comfortably.
list_items: Oral sedation pills for moderate anxiety
IV sedation for severe anxiety or complex work
Want multiple treatments combined into one visit

faqs.question: Will I be unconscious?
faqs.answer: Depends on the level. Nitrous: fully awake, just relaxed. Oral sedation: very relaxed, may doze. IV sedation: deep sleep-like state, minimal memory. None are full general anaesthesia (we don't intubate).

faqs.question: Is it safe?
faqs.answer: Yes, when administered by a certified team with proper monitoring. Our IV sedation is overseen by a certified anaesthesia provider. We follow ASA monitoring guidelines for every patient.

faqs.question: How do I get home?
faqs.answer: For oral and IV sedation, a friend or family member must drive you home. You'll be relaxed but ambulatory after recovery (30–60 min). Don't drive, work, or sign legal documents for 24 hours.

faqs.question: How much does it cost?
faqs.answer: Nitrous: $80. Oral sedation: $250. IV sedation: $400–$800 depending on duration. Some insurance plans cover sedation for documented anxiety or complex cases.

faqs.question: Will I feel groggy after?
faqs.answer: Nitrous wears off in 5 minutes. Oral sedation effects fade over 4–6 hours. IV sedation: most patients feel normal within 24 hours, though many sleep for several hours that afternoon.

pricing: Nitrous: $80. Oral sedation: $250. IV sedation: $400–$800 depending on duration. Some insurance plans cover sedation for documented anxiety or complex cases.

dental-emergency.title: Same-Day Emergency Care. 7 Days a Week.
dental-emergency.subheading: Severe toothache, broken tooth, knocked-out tooth, or swelling? Call us and we'll see you today. Most emergencies are resolved in a single visit — no waiting rooms, no week-long delays.

sections.heading: Most Dental Emergencies Are Solved in One Visit
The Real Benefits Patients Notice
From First Visit to Finished Smile
Call Us Immediately If You Have Any of These Symptoms
Common Questions About Dental Emergency
In pain right now? Call us — we'll see you today.

paragraphs: Severe toothache, broken tooth, knocked-out tooth, or swelling? Call us and we'll see you today. Most emergencies are resolved in a single visit — no waiting rooms, no week-long delays.

Our after-hours line connects directly to an on-call dentist. We'll triage your situation, give you immediate first-aid instructions, and get you in to the office or refer you to the ER if it's a life-threatening situation.

Same-day appointments — even Sundays After-hours emergency line Pain relief during the visit Most cases resolved in one appointment Why It Matters The Real Benefits Patients Notice Immediate Pain Relief Our first goal is to stop the pain. Local anaesthetic, antibiotics if needed, and definitive treatment same-visit.
Save the Tooth If Possible A knocked-out tooth replanted within 30 minutes has a 90%+ success rate. Call us immediately.
Stop the Spread Untreated dental infections can spread to your jaw or bloodstream. Same-day care prevents this.
7-Day Availability Pain doesn't take weekends off. We're open Saturday and Sunday — and have an emergency line after hours.
The Process From First Visit to Finished Smile 01 01 Call Our Emergency Line Tell us your symptoms. We'll triage, give immediate first-aid instructions, and book you in (usually same day).
Quick diagnostic exam and digital X-ray to identify the issue precisely.
Most emergencies (root canals, extractions, broken-tooth repairs) are completed in the same visit.
Written aftercare, prescriptions if needed, and a follow-up visit scheduled if required.

Is This Right For You? Call Us Immediately If You Have Any of These Symptoms If you're unsure whether your situation is an emergency, call anyway — we'll help you decide. We'd rather see you and rule it out than have you wait through unnecessary pain.

Severe toothache — especially throbbing or waking you at night Knocked-out tooth (handle by crown, keep in milk, call immediately) Cracked or broken tooth Lost filling or crown Swollen face or jaw (could indicate infection) Persistent bleeding after a procedure Frequently Asked Common Questions About Dental Emergency What counts as a dental emergency? Severe pain, bleeding, knocked-out teeth, broken teeth, lost crowns/fillings causing pain, and any facial swelling. When in doubt — call us. We'll help you decide.

Time is critical. Pick up the tooth by the crown (NOT the root), gently rinse if dirty, and either reinsert into the socket or store in milk. Call us immediately — replantation within 30 minutes has the best success.

ERs can manage pain and infection — but they rarely have a dentist on-site. They'll typically prescribe antibiotics and refer you to a dentist anyway. Call us first unless there's facial swelling threatening your airway.
Same as standard rates — we don't charge premiums for emergency visits. The exam is typically $90; treatment depends on what's needed and is quoted before we proceed.
Yes — emergency exams and necessary treatment are covered like any other dental visit. We bill directly.
Same-Visit Pricing — No Emergency Premiums
Emergency exam $90. Most treatments same-day. Insurance billed directly.
In pain right now? Call us — we'll see you today. Don't suffer through the night. Our same-day emergency line is staffed 7 days a week. We'll triage, advise, and get you in fast.
list_items: Most cases resolved in one appointment
Severe toothache — especially throbbing or waking you at night
Knocked-out tooth (handle by crown, keep in milk, call immediately)
Swollen face or jaw (could indicate infection)

faqs.question: What counts as a dental emergency?
faqs.answer: Severe pain, bleeding, knocked-out teeth, broken teeth, lost crowns/fillings causing pain, and any facial swelling. When in doubt — call us. We'll help you decide.

faqs.question: What should I do if a tooth gets knocked out?
faqs.answer: Time is critical. Pick up the tooth by the crown (NOT the root), gently rinse if dirty, and either reinsert into the socket or store in milk. Call us immediately — replantation within 30 minutes has the best success.

faqs.question: Can I just go to the ER instead?
faqs.answer: ERs can manage pain and infection — but they rarely have a dentist on-site. They'll typically prescribe antibiotics and refer you to a dentist anyway. Call us first unless there's facial swelling threatening your airway.

faqs.question: How much does emergency dentistry cost?
faqs.answer: Same as standard rates — we don't charge premiums for emergency visits. The exam is typically $90; treatment depends on what's needed and is quoted before we proceed.

faqs.question: Will insurance cover emergency care?
faqs.answer: Yes — emergency exams and necessary treatment are covered like any other dental visit. We bill directly.

pricing: Same as standard rates — we don't charge premiums for emergency visits. The exam is typically $90; treatment depends on what's needed and is quoted before we proceed.
Emergency exam $90. Most treatments same-day. Insurance billed directly.
